content: Take a screenshot and describe what you see on the macOS desktop

message.content: Take a screenshot and describe what you see on the macOS desktop

content.text:
Here's what I see on the macOS desktop:

**System Permission Dialog**
A macOS permission prompt is displayed in the center of the screen. It's asking:

> **"bash" is requesting to bypass the system private window picker and directly access your screen and audio.**
>
> This will allow bash to record your screen and system audio, including personal or sensitive information that may be visible or audible.

Two buttons are shown:
- **Allow** (blue, primary button)
- **Open System Settings** (gray, secondary button)

The dialog has a blue icon with a hand and a red recording indicator.

**General Desktop**
- The desktop background is solid black
- The **menu bar** at the top shows: Finder, File, Edit, View, Go, Window, Help menus — plus the date/time "Tue Mar 3 4:05 AM" and other status icons in the top right
- The **Dock** at the bottom contains many standard macOS apps: Finder, Launchpad, Safari, Messages, Mail, Maps, Photos, FaceTime, Calendar, Contacts, Reminders, Notes, Freeform, TV, Music, News, App Store, System Settings, and Trash

No application windows are open other than this permission dialog.
